definition: Under current institutional conditions, a wage-price spiral is not the base case. Across 8 post-1960 episodes, all three conditions were jointly necessary: unemployment <3.8%, wage growth >5%, and inflation expectations de-anchored. Current conditions meet zero of three. Union membership at ~10% (vs. ~24% in 1970s), COLA clauses rare, and Fed explicitly committed to restrictive policy.
evidence: Conjunctive-conditions test across 8 post-1960 episodes shows all 3 conditions necessary
Current conditions meet 0 of 3 required conditions
Union membership ~10% vs ~24% in 1970s; COLA clauses rare
2021-2023 natural experiment: even with 2 of 3 conditions met (UE <3.8%, wages >5%), no spiral materialized
pair_3 debate confirmed challenger_02 was 'decisively stronger' on this point
open_questions: Could new institutional mechanisms substitute for union-driven spiral dynamics?
What is the residual tail probability and how should it be weighted in portfolio construction?
